perturbation.original_text: 1.27 Maintenance Services - the services described in SCHEDULE D or in any Product License Schedule or Order with respect to any Licensed Program including telephone consultation, online and on-site technical support, Error correction and the provision of Updates.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.27 Support Services - the services described in SCHEDULE D or in any Product License Schedule or Order with respect to any Licensed Program including telephone consultation, online and on-site technical support, Error correction and the provision of Updates.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Maintenance Services' is a key term defined and used consistently throughout the contract. By changing the definition of 'Maintenance Services' to 'Support Services' in section 1.27, while leaving the original term elsewhere, this creates an internal contradiction. This is specifically a 'Misaligned Terminology' issue because the contract later refers to 'Maintenance Services' in other sections, creating uncertainty on which definition to use. This confusion particularly impacts sections dealing with fees and obligations related to 'Maintenance Services'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.0 MAINTENANCE SERVICES 11.1 Supplier shall provide the Maintenance Services attached hereto as SCHEDULE D.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Maintenance Services'. The changed text redefines 'Maintenance Services' as 'Support Services' while the contract elsewhere refers to 'Maintenance Services' (as shown in the contradicted text: '11.0 MAINTENANCE SERVICES'). This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty because the contract uses the term 'Maintenance Services' in a later section, leading to ambiguity about which definition applies. The contract explicitly promises that 'Supplier shall provide the Maintenance Services attached hereto as SCHEDULE D', which is now contradicted because the term has been redefined as 'Support Services' in 1.27. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 1.13 Customizations - modifications to the Licensed Programs and new coding made at the request or Bank of America.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.13 Adaptations - modifications to the Licensed Programs and new coding made at the request or Bank of America.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the key term 'Customizations' to 'Adaptations' in Section 1.13 introduces a contradiction. The contract refers to customizations in numerous places, especially when detailing rights, ownership, and payment. Altering the name in just this definition introduces significant ambiguity. This specifically causes in-text contradictions with sections discussing specifics about Customizations, ownership, and payment structures because the agreement fails to identify 'Adaptations' and 'Customizations' as the same thing. Thus, when 'Customization' is mentioned later on, there will be uncertainity on the meaning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.0 CUSTOMIZATIONS 7.1 Supplier shall provide Bank of America, within twenty-one (21) calendar days after receipt of the Bank of America's request setting forth the relevant requirements, with a written estimate of the cost of the Customizations. Bank of America may direct Supplier to provide such written estimate on a time and materials basis or a fixed price basis, and Supplier shall comply with such direction. Supplier's response shall set forth the Delivery Target Date for such Customizations.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Customizations' to 'Adaptations' introduces a strong in-text contradiction. Section 7.0 explicitly refers to 'CUSTOMIZATIONS' and outlines the process for estimating their cost and delivery. By changing the definition of 'Customizations' to 'Adaptations' in Section 1.13, the contract creates ambiguity as to whether Section 7.0 still applies. If 'Customizations' and 'Adaptations' are not the same, then Section 7.0 has no process for 'Adaptations', thus making it uncertain how the cost and delivery will be handled, leading to a strong contradiction. This change directly impacts the enforceability and clarity of the agreement regarding customizations as described in section 7.0.

perturbation.original_text: 1.41 Software - the Licensed Programs and Object Code licensed by Supplier pursuant to a Product License Schedule that produces the results described in the Program Materials, together with the Documentation, all Corrections, Customizations and Updates and any Upgrades acquired by Bank of America pursuant to this Agreement, and, if licensed to Bank of America in this Agreement, the Source Code or other software programs offered by Supplier to the public on Supplier's Web site and used by Bank of America, notwithstanding any associated EULA, GPL or other license terms, any Updates thereto, and any related user manuals or Documentation.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.41 Programs - the Licensed Programs and Object Code licensed by Supplier pursuant to a Product License Schedule that produces the results described in the Program Materials, together with the Documentation, all Corrections, Customizations and Updates and any Upgrades acquired by Bank of America pursuant to this Agreement, and, if licensed to Bank of America in this Agreement, the Source Code or other software programs offered by Supplier to the public on Supplier's Web site and used by Bank of America, notwithstanding any associated EULA, GPL or other license terms, any Updates thereto, and any related user manuals or Documentation.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of 'Software' to 'Programs' in section 1.41 will cause issues throughout the legal text due to the amount of times 'Software' is being used. This means that anytime software is being referenced in any other section, it will cause uncertainty to the meaning of 'Software'. The usage of 'Programs' is never used anywhere else in the document, leading to an In-Text contradiction issue.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Supplier hereby grants Bank of America a nonexclusive, worldwide, irrevocable, perpetual license to install, use, execute and copy the Software described in each Product License Schedule as necessary to conduct Bank of America business in accordance with the terms and restrictions of this Section and any special terms and restrictions stated on the applicable Product License Schedule.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Software' to 'Programs' in the definition of section 1.41 creates a direct conflict with section 2.1. Section 2.1 explicitly refers to 'Software' and grants a license for its use. By changing the defined term from 'Software' to 'Programs', the contract now lacks a clear definition of what 'Software' refers to in section 2.1. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty, potentially rendering the license grant unenforceable because the subject matter is not clearly defined. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists as defined in the Perturbation Explanations.
